fields.note: I'll own src/jinja2/ext.py and implement both my trans_metadata feature and your domain= feature. Please send SPEC for exact behavior needed (parse() parameter detection, _install_callables()/_make_node() signature changes, error handling, defaults).

content:
type: DEFER
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
spec: Add optional translation-metadata params to the trans tag, parsed in InternationalizationExtension.parse() and stored via _make_node().

WHERE IN parse(): while parsing the trans tag's parameter list (the loop that currently handles plural_expr/variables/trimmed/notrimmed identifiers before the block content), add detection for three special identifier names: note, created, modified. Introduce 'metadata: t.Dict[str, str] = {}' near where other parse-state locals (e.g. trimmed, plural_expr) are initialized.

DETECTION LOGIC: when the parser sees an identifier token whose value is one of note/created/modified (check via parser.stream.current.value or similar, same way trimmed/notrimmed and plural_expr assignment-targets are currently detected), treat it specially instead of falling through to the generic variable-name handling:
  1. Advance past the identifier.
  2. Require an '=' (assign) token next. If not present, raise self.fail(f"metadata parameter '{name}' requires a value assignment.") -- self.fail wraps TemplateAssertionError with lineno, consistent with existing error calls in this method.
  3. Parse the value with parser.parse_expression() (or equivalent already used for default values elsewhere in this method).
  4. If the parsed node is not a nodes.Const with a str value (i.e. not a plain string literal), raise self.fail(f"metadata parameter '{name}' must be a string literal.").
  5. Store metadata[name] = <the string value> and continue the loop (do not treat it as a normal template variable / do not add it to the variables dict used for gettext args).
  6. Any identifier NOT in {note, created, modified} keeps falling through to today's existing behavior untouched (regular variable, trimmed/notrimmed, pluralize handling, etc.) -- do not change existing parsing paths for those.
  7. Metadata params may appear in ANY position/order relative to variables, trimmed/notrimmed -- don't assume they're first or last in the comma-separated list.

_make_node(): add a new parameter 'metadata: t.Dict[str, str]' to the method signature (give it a sensible position, e.g. keyword-only after existing params, whatever matches your merged signature best -- just make sure every call site to _make_node() from parse() passes the metadata dict collected above, defaulting to {} if none was parsed). After the method builds its 'output_node' (the Output node it currently returns / assigns before return), add:
    if metadata:
        output_node.trans_metadata = metadata
Do NOT set the attribute when metadata is empty (nodes without metadata must not have a 'trans_metadata' attribute at all -- no hasattr should return True for plain trans tags).

CONSTRAINTS:
- Must not change render output at all -- purely an AST-attached, extraction/tooling-facing attribute.
- Must not break babel_extract or existing pluralization/trimmed/variable handling.
- Works with trimmed/notrimmed and pluralize in any combination and any parameter order, e.g.: {% trans note="x", user_count, trimmed %}...{% endtrans %}, {% trans note="a", created="2024-01-01" %}...{% pluralize %}...{% endtrans %}.
- Empty string metadata values ({% trans note="" %}) are valid and must still set trans_metadata (non-empty dict check is on the dict itself, i.e. whether any keys were collected, not on the string values).
- Unicode/very long strings in metadata values must work (they're just string literals from parser.parse_expression()/Const, no special handling needed).
- Error messages must be exactly: "metadata parameter '{name}' must be a string literal." and "metadata parameter '{name}' requires a value assignment." (with the actual param name substituted for {name}).
note: Full spec for trans_metadata feature - please implement alongside your domain= work. I will not touch ext.py.

note: FYI: your changes are not visible in my sandbox (separate filesystem/git tree) - git status stayed clean and no patch.txt appeared here after your DONE.
